channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Overgrown garden affecting public footpath and shared access, 18th July
item.description:
I would like to report an overgrown front garden at a property on Oak road. The front and back gardens have become severely overgrown. Trees and vegetation from the front garden are extending onto the public footpath, making it difficult for pedestrians to pass safely. Weeds and other vegetation are also obstructing the shared side access between the properties. Please could the council inspect the property and take the appropriate action to ensure the public footpath and shared access are kept clear
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Oak Road, Slade Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cyclist safety as the traffic lights are green for incoming traffic, 18th July
item.description:
Since the completion of recent roadworks, the current traffic signal phasing forces a direct conflict between southbound cyclists with a green light and right-turning vehicles. This is a hazardous design flaw that will inevitably lead to a serious collision unless rectified immediately. I was nearly getting hit by a vehicle turning right onto Outer Circle as I was going straight and had and traffic light for cyclist was green. Drivers see the road leading to the bridge as a one way road ,but don't realize there is a cycle lane coming down. I would appreciate an update on the assessment timeline for this junction
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Outer Circle, Marylebone, City of Westminster
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside 83 Glengall Road on corner following on from Haselmere Road, 18th July
item.description:
Another trip hazard with paving stone cracked and lifting up.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Glengall Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Not working, 18th July
item.description:
Not working
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Sweeps Lane, St Mary Cray, Greater London

item.title: Partial chest of draws dumped, 18th July
item.description:
2nd time reporting as you closed the last one as fixed. It’s not fixed and the items still remain

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Maxey Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Light not working, 18th July
item.description:
Not working
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 163, Star Lane, St Paul's Cray, Orpington, Greater London

item.title: Bin bags/rubbish on pavement obstructing pavement., 18th July
item.description:
Bin bags/rubbish on pavement obstructing pavement. Both disgusting and dangerous. Pavements are narrow and pedestrians will have to walk in road to avoid.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lubbock Road, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Further dumping of bikes, 18th July
item.description:
There have been numerous bikes here all week they are not being collected

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Worsley Bridge Road, New Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Vehicle stored on public road sold for parts, 17th July
item.description:
Vehicle, looks like it was involved in a crash has been parked on the street. Air bags are popped. Now front seats are missing. Looks like someone is selling the car for parts whilst is parked on the public road.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tyeshurst Close, Upper Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: “Wait” button is not working, 17th July
item.description:
This is for crossing the southmost strip of old Kent Road, just south of where Dunton. Road meets old Kent road. I push the button and the pedestrian signal does not light uo or otherwise show any sign of responding. However the pedestrian light did change to green

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 278-280, Old Kent Road, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Water coming through road surface on Wickham Way, 17th July
item.description:
First noticed on 16th July that water appeared to be coming up through the road surface on Wickham Way outside 155/157. This has continued today and appears to have led to some minor degradation of the road surface as vehicles have driven through it.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 153, Wickham Way, Park Langley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: No space for wheelchair users or visually impaired, 17th July
item.description:
No space for wheelchair users or visually impaired

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Northern Drive, Bethnal Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Delivery drivers blocking pavement frequently, 17th July
item.description:
Delivery riders’ motorcycles are regularly being parked on the pavement outside GDK, obstructing the public footway. As shown in the attached photograph, the motorcycles reduce the available space for pedestrians, creating difficulties for wheelchair users, people with pushchairs, and those with mobility impairments. This appears to be a recurring issue associated with food delivery collections at this location. I request that the council investigate and take appropriate enforcement action to prevent pavement obstruction and ensure the public footway remains clear and accessible for all users. This is a persistent problem rather than an isolated incident, with delivery riders regularly using the pavement as a parking area while waiting to collect orders.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 267, Walworth Road, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bags of rubbish dumped at the bus stop, 17th July
item.description:
Everyday now people are dumping bags of rubbish at the bus stop! The foxes are then getting hold of the bags and make a mess everywhere! It was a man on a bike that dumped this bag.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Leesons Hill, St Paul's Cray, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 4 big bags of commercial waste dumped by shahs halal food, 17th July
item.description:
Commercial waste bags have been dumped on the public highway by Shah’s Halal Food. As shown in the attached photograph, multiple commercial refuse sacks have been left on the pavement instead of being stored or presented correctly for collection. There is also litter scattered along the street, much of which appears to be fast-food packaging from nearby takeaway businesses. This is creating an unsightly environment, attracting vermin, obstructing the pavement, and posing a public health risk. I request that the council investigate the commercial waste management practices of the businesses in this area, including Shah’s Halal Food, and take appropriate enforcement action where breaches are identified.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 267, Walworth Road, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Granville road opposite William house, 17th July
item.description:
Street light lump out for a long time. It is very dark
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Granville Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road works not completed, 17th July
item.description:
The road works and associated road closure were scheduled to finish on 17 July at 5 pm, yet the road remains closed. Bus routes are still being diverted, and road‑work materials have been left abandoned in the area. This prolonged disruption is unacceptable. Please provide a clear explanation for the delay and confirm when the road will be fully reopened.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rosebery Avenue, Holborn, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Frequent dog foul and urine, 17th July
item.description:
Frequent dog foul and urine. need cleaning and signs

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 15, Albert Road, Ramsden, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Traffic cones used to reserve parking, 17th July
item.description:
Outside 33 Bowen Drive two yellow traffic cones are frequently used to reserve parking.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bowen Drive, Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter along footpath and kerb area, 17th July
item.description:
I have reported excess litter three times now and receive an email to say it has been fixed but it clearly hasn’t. The rubbish in this road is constant due to the traffic of cars and pedestrians who have no litter bins so just throw their litter on the road and it is rarely cleared so builds up

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 7, Blyth Road, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
